title: Does the cyclic group C_9423 admit a (9423,4711,2355)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean is a secondary formal-seal lane for this packet, not the gate to publication status. The bounded publication audit supports a human-ready exact-case note: Baumert-Gordon 2004 still anchors (9423,4711,2355) as open, Gordon's 2019 small-open-case slide still includes 9423, and a bounded 2026 follow-up status check surfaced the same seven survivors. Formalization can proceed later as archival sealing once the short note text is frozen.
micro_paper_assessment: Passes the strict micro-paper lane on bounded publication audit. The packet closes a named residual cyclic Hadamard case with a short structural nonexistence proof, the exact and alternate-notation prior-art checks did not surface a later settlement, and the remaining work is bounded to citation pinning and short-note packaging rather than new mathematics.
family_affinity: high; exact residual cyclic Hadamard case with a family-typical multiplier/quotient obstruction
generalization_signal: There is a genuine local pattern via the 2-multiplier and the order-3 quotient, but only the exact 9423 case was verified here. The broader slice may overlap known folklore and needs a separate novelty check before packaging.
strongest_honest_claim: The bounded verify and publication audit support the exact nonexistence theorem that C_9423 admits no (9423,4711,2355)-difference set. Baumert-Gordon 2004 still anchors the row as open, Gordon's 2019 cyclic-Hadamard survivor slide and a bounded 2026 follow-up status check still carry 9423 among the same seven unresolved values, and the preserved proof gives a short structural contradiction via the 2-multiplier, the index-3 quotient, and the forced integral order-3 character value with square 2356.
paper_title_hint: On the Nonexistence of a Cyclic (9423,4711,2355)-Difference Set
candidate_theorem_slice: Exact audited slice: there is no cyclic (9423,4711,2355)-difference set in C_9423; equivalently, after 2-multiplier normalization, the mod-3 quotient profile must have equal nonzero classes, forcing a nonprincipal order-3 character value to be an integer whose square would have to equal 2356.
next_action: This packet is HUMAN_READY and has been moved to LEAN_QUEUE for formal sealing; do not block fresh curation/solve work on pending Lean.
